description:
You are an IT-support technician who has just received a small queue of internal help-desk tickets that all concern the in-house “pkgdb” application-package repository.  
The company keeps every locally built package as an **empty directory** inside

    /home/user/pkgdb/

and uses a very simple naming convention:

    {package-name}-{semantic-version}

For example, the directory “/home/user/pkgdb/alpha-1.0.0” represents version 1.0.0 of package “alpha”.

A CSV file with the current support tickets is already present at

    /home/user/tickets/ticket_queue.csv

Format of that file  
(Comma-separated, **no extra spaces**, one line per ticket):

TicketID,Package,RequiredVersion,Action  
101,alpha,1.2.0,upgrade  
102,beta,N/A,remove  
103,delta,4.5.6,install  
104,gamma,0.9.0,downgrade  

Current repository status  
(what is on disk right now, before you start):

/home/user/pkgdb/alpha-1.0.0  
/home/user/pkgdb/beta-2.3.4  
/home/user/pkgdb/gamma-0.9.8  

Your tasks

1. Read the ticket queue and carry out every requested action by manipulating the directories inside /home/user/pkgdb/ **only**.  
   • upgrade or downgrade → delete the existing directory for that package (if any) and create a new empty directory whose name matches the RequiredVersion.  
   • install → simply create the new directory (the package is currently absent).  
   • remove → delete the directory (no directory for that package may remain).

2. Produce a resolution log file named

    /home/user/tickets/resolution_log.csv

Exact log file format (this is what the automated grader will check):  
• File must use UTF-8 encoding, Unix line-endings (LF).  
• The very first line must be the header shown below.  
• There must be exactly one subsequent line per ticket, **in the same order as in ticket_queue.csv**.  
• Each line must contain exactly five comma-separated fields, with no surrounding spaces:

TicketID,Package,Action,ResultVersion,Status

Field meanings:  
TicketID      → copy from the ticket queue.  
Package       → copy from the ticket queue.  
Action        → copy from the ticket queue.  
ResultVersion → the version that now exists in /home/user/pkgdb/ after your work.  
                For a remove action, use the literal “N/A”.  
Status        → write “completed”.

Example of the first two data lines (your final file must of course show the real versions that you ended up with):

101,alpha,upgrade,1.2.0,completed  
102,beta,remove,N/A,completed

3. When all tickets are processed, the directory /home/user/pkgdb/ must contain **exactly and only** the directories that correspond to the post-resolution versions.

Do not move, rename, or delete any other files or directories outside /home/user/pkgdb/ and /home/user/tickets/.
truth:
Expected state AFTER the agent finishes:

1.  /home/user/pkgdb/ must contain exactly these three empty directories (no others):
    • /home/user/pkgdb/alpha-1.2.0
    • /home/user/pkgdb/delta-4.5.6
    • /home/user/pkgdb/gamma-0.9.0

   Directories that MUST NOT exist any longer:
    • /home/user/pkgdb/alpha-1.0.0
    • /home/user/pkgdb/beta-2.3.4
    • /home/user/pkgdb/gamma-0.9.8

2.  /home/user/tickets/resolution_log.csv must exist and contain exactly five LF-terminated lines:

TicketID,Package,Action,ResultVersion,Status
101,alpha,upgrade,1.2.0,completed
102,beta,remove,N/A,completed
103,delta,install,4.5.6,completed
104,gamma,downgrade,0.9.0,completed

3.  Permissions: all newly created directories and the log file should inherit default user permissions; the grader will only check existence and content, not mode bits.

4.  Original files supplied before the task starts (for fixture setup by test harness):

    /home/user/tickets/ticket_queue.csv  with the exact content already shown in the public task description.
    Existing pkgdb directories:
       /home/user/pkgdb/alpha-1.0.0   (pre-created, empty)
       /home/user/pkgdb/beta-2.3.4    (pre-created, empty)
       /home/user/pkgdb/gamma-0.9.8   (pre-created, empty)

No other directories are present in /home/user/pkgdb/ at start.

The grader will verify:
• presence/absence of the directories listed above,
• exact byte-for-byte content of /home/user/tickets/resolution_log.csv.
